name: L-Sit Leg Raises
description: Hold your body weight on your hands with your legs extended and feet on the ground. Raise each foot individually

name: Chest Level
description: Instead of moving forwards until your hands are at your waist position, only move forwards until your hands are at chest position

name: Chest Level
description: Move your hands all the way down to your waist

name: Bent Knee
description: Start from a kneeling position and keep your knees bent as you lift. This reduces the lever and makes it easier to assume the position

name: Straddle
description: Perform the lever with legs straddled apart to keep your centre of gravity closer to your hands

name: Floor
description: Get into the position and hold

name: Elevated
description: Place your feet on a raised surface which will put more body weight onto the shoulders

name: Floor Push Up
description: From the pike position bend your elbows until your head is just above the ground

name: Elevated Push Up
description: From the elevated pike position bend your elbows until your head is just above the ground

name: Negative
description: From a planche lean position, perform the downards portion of the exercise without pushing back up

name: Chest Level
description: Move forwards until your hands are at chest position and perform the the push up

name: Wall
description: With your stomach against the wall, perform the push up. Using the wall will remove the balance aspect while building the required strength

name: Straddle Leg raises
description: Place your hands on the bar in the flag position and hop your legs off the ground into a straddle flag position then return back to the ground. Allow your lower foot to not raise as high

name: Leg raises
description: Perform like the straddle flag leg raise but bring your feet together at the top to briefly be in the flag position

name: Straddle Flag
description: Perform a human flag with your legs apart rather than together

name: Half Archer Pull Up
description: Do the archer pull up with your hands at should width and pull to one side.

name: Tuck
description: Perform the windshield wiper with your legs tucked in

name: Leg raises
description: From the hold position, raise your upper leg

name: Chest
description: Continue the pull up until your chest is level with the bar

name: Stomach
description: Continue the pull up until your stomach is level with the bar

name: Hips
description: Continue the pull up until your hips are level with the bar

name: Chest Dip
description: Lean your torso forward at a 30 to 45 degree angle with your knees bent to focus on the chest muscles

name: Triceps Dip
description: Keep your torso completely upright and perpendicular to the floor with your legs straight to focus on the triceps

name: One Leg L-Sit Kicks
description: Do the L sit kicks one leg at a time to lower the difficulty

name: Knees Outside
description: Rest your knees on the outside of your arms with your elbows bent

name: Crow Pose
description: Place your knees behind your triceps with your elbows bent

name: Straight Arms
description: Place your knees behind your triceps and straighten your arms

name: Elevated
description: Place your hand on an elevated surface to get used to the movement with lower resistance

name: Assisted
description: Place your other hand on a raised surface to provide balance and support while training the strength of the other arm

name: Advanced
description: Rather than tucking your knees into your chest, move your legs backwards so your thighs are perpendicular to the floor

name: Advanced tuck
description: Rather than tucking your knees into your chest, move your legs backwards so your thighs are perpendicular to the floor

name: Straddle
description: Perform the planche with your legs straddled apart rather than together
